news.summary: The Board of Directors at Kohl’s Corporation recently announced a quarterly dividend of $0.125 per share on the Company’s common stock. The dividend will be paid out on June 25, 2025, to shareholders of record as of June 11, 2025. Kohl’s (NYSE: KSS) is a prominent omnichannel retailer known for offering a mix of top brands, excellent value, and convenience to its customers.
news.headline: Kohl’s Corporation declares a quarterly dividend of $0.125 per share on common stock, payable on June 25, 2025.

news.summary: Athens, Greece - On May 14, 2025, Okeanis Eco Tankers Corp. (NYSE: ECO / OSE: OET) disclosed that its board of directors has approved a dividend for its common shares. The company, also known as OET, made this announcement today.

news.summary: In Omaha, Nebraska, Boston Omaha Corporation (NYSE: BOC) has released its financial results for the first quarter ending on March 31, 2025, alongside the submission of its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q to the Securities and Exchange Commission. Key financial data for the first quarters of 2025 and 2024 is outlined below, and for more detailed information, the complete Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q can be accessed at www.bostonomaha.com. Additionally, a supplementary presentation with further financial details is available for review. Visit the company's website for more insights.

news.summary: In Bristol, Pennsylvania on May 14, 2025, TESCO Metering celebrates becoming one of the initial manufacturers to be certified under the National Electrical Manufacturers Association's (NEMA) fresh Make It American™ initiative. This recognition highlights TESCO's commitment to domestic manufacturing. They join as a pilot partner in the program, showcasing their dedication to promoting American-made products.
news.headline: TESCO Metering recognized as pilot partner in NEMA's Make It American™ Program, emphasizing US manufacturing.

news.summary: Contango ORE, Inc. announced their Q1-2025 financial results, revealing total income of $19.3 million compared to a $2.9 million loss in Q1-2024. They reported a net loss of $22.3 million for Q1-2025, inclusive of a $40.5 million non-cash expense from derivative contracts. Gold sales in Q1-2025 amounted to 17,382 ounces with Cash Costs of $1,334 per ounce and AISC of $1,374 per ounce. Their unrestricted cash position as of March 31, 2025, was $35.0 million, up from $20.1 million in December 2024. The Company's Q1-2025 net cash from operating activities was $28.6 million, a notable improvement from a $4.4 million use in Q1-2024. They experienced zero cash used in investing activities in Q1-2025, a change from $15.5 million in Q1-2024. Cash used in financing activities was $13.7 million in Q1-2025.Contango mentioned significant progress in various projects, including the successful production of gold exceeding quarterly guidance, reducing hedge balance, and initiation of processing in the Manh Choh second campaign. The Company received cash distributions of $24 million in Q1-2025 and an additional $9 million in April 2025. They also reported the completion of a Technical Report Summary on the Johnson Tract Project, showcasing robust economics. The Company aims to focus on permitting related to this project.
news.headline: Contango made $19.3M in Q1-2025, $22.3M net loss due to $40.5M unrealized loss on derivative contracts. Sold 17,382 ounces of gold with cash costs per ounce of $1,334. Unrestricted cash position at $35M on March 31, 2025. Company received $24M in cash distributions in Q1-2025. Placed 15,000 ounces production in Contango's account for the second campaign of 2025. Commission dismissed lawsuit against the State of Alaska DOT, enhancing impacts for Manh Choh project. NPV over $400M for Johnson Tract Project. Net cash provided from operating activities up at $28.6M in Q1-2025. Statement of Loss was $22.3M in Q1-2025.

news.summary: Edesa Biotech, Inc. reported its financial results for the three and six months ending March 31, 2025. The company completed a $15 million equity financing, focusing on the development of their vitiligo drug candidate EB06. Total operating expenses decreased to $1.6 million for the three months and $3.5 million for the six months. The company had cash and cash equivalents of $13.9 million and a working capital of $13.5 million. Edesa plans to submit drug manufacturing data to the FDA in the second half of 2025, with anticipated topline results in 12-18 months. The company is focused on developing treatments for immune-related diseases and has upcoming clinical studies for vitiligo and pulmonary fibrosis. The CEO highlighted the potential impact of their drug on vitiligo patients worldwide. The company aims to transform treatment paradigms through innovative immunotherapies. Financial results showed a net loss of $1.6 million for the quarter ending March 31, 2025, and $3.2 million for the six months. Edesa Biotech anticipates a positive future despite known and unknown risks associated with product development and commercialization. They will be participating in the BIO International Convention in June 2025 for networking and business opportunities.
news.headline: Edesa Biotech reports $15M equity financing, $13.9M cash on hand, focusing on vitiligo drug development. Net loss $3.2M in 6 months.

news.summary: Stellus Private Credit BDC reported strong financial results for the fourth fiscal quarter ended March 31, 2025. The company generated $0.31 per share of net investment income and maintained a stable net asset value. They funded $37 million of investments and received $4 million in repayments, bringing the total portfolio to $335 million at fair value. For the quarter, investments at fair value totaled $334.7 million, with total assets at $339.5 million. The weighted average yield of debt and other income-producing investments was 10.6%, and the weighted average yield on total investments was 10.0%. Investment income for the quarter was $8.1 million, with gross operating expenses at $5.3 million. The company's net investment income was $3.3 million, resulting in a net asset value of $165.48 per share. The company had $121.8 million in outstanding borrowings under the Credit Facility and $50.0 million under the SPV Facility. Distributions for the quarter totaled $0.36 per share. Stellus Private Credit BDC made several new investments in various companies during the period, showcasing its investment activity and portfolio diversification strategy.
news.headline: Stellus Private Credit BDC reports $0.31 per share net investment income, $37M funded investments, $335M total portfolio.

news.summary: In Torrance, California on May 14, 2025, Navitas Semiconductor (Nasdaq: NVTS) made public that Cristiano Amoruso is joining their board of directors right away. Navitas Semiconductor is known for its specialization in GaN power ICs and SiC technology as the sole pure-play next-gen power semiconductor firm. This addition to their board is a significant move for the company as it continues to expand its leadership team in the industry.
news.headline: Navitas Semiconductor appoints Cristiano Amoruso to board of directors. Cristiano to join NVTS board effective immediately.

news.summary: In Perrysburg, Ohio, on May 14, 2025, O-I Glass, Inc. (NYSE: OI) announced that Alan J. Murray is retiring from the company's Board of Directors post the Annual Meeting of Share Owners. Murray, a long-serving member of the Board, is stepping down after years of service. Additionally, at the same meeting, 11 director nominees were elected for one-year terms. Among the newly elected members are Eugenio Garza y Garza and Iain J. Mackay. The other directors elected are Samuel R. Chapin, David V. Clark, II, Eric J. Foss, Gordon J. Hardie, John Humphrey, Hari N. Nair, Cheri Phyfer, Catherine I. Slater, and Carol A. Williams.
news.headline: Alan J. Murray retiring from O-I board; Eugenio Garza y Garza and Iain J. Mackay elected. 11 directors elected.

news.summary: Nextracker, a prominent solar technology platform provider listed on Nasdaq under the symbol NXT, recently disclosed its financial performance for the fourth quarter and complete fiscal year 2025, concluding on March 31, 2025. In Q4 FY25, the company reported revenue of $924 million, marking an increase from $737 million in Q4 FY24 and up from $679 million in Q3 FY25. The GAAP Gross Profit stood at $306 million in Q4 FY25, compared to $340 million in Q4 FY24 and $241 million in Q3 FY25, with the GAAP Gross Margin reported at 33.1%, sliding from 46.2% in Q4 FY24 and 35.5% in Q3 FY25. On the bottom line, Nextracker recorded GAAP Net Income of $158 million in Q4 FY25, down from $223 million in Q4 FY24 and up from $117 million in Q3 FY25, with GAAP Net Income Margin at 17.1% in Q4 FY25, dropping from 30.3% in Q4 FY24 and matching the 17.3% in Q3 FY25. The GAAP Diluted EPS was $1.05 in Q4 FY25, compared to $0.79 in Q3 FY25.
news.headline: Nextracker (Nasdaq: NXT) reports Q4 FY25 revenue of $924 million with 33.1% gross margin and $158 million net income.

news.summary: In Fremont, California, Nextracker (Nasdaq: NXT) revealed its acquisition of Bentek Corporation, a U.S.-based company known for creating electrical infrastructure for various solar power plants. The acquisition, valued at about $78 million in cash with potential additional earnout considerations, brings together Bentek's pre-assembled eBOS solutions with Nextracker's renowned solar tracker platform. This move is expected to enhance Nextracker's position in the solar technology industry.
news.headline: Nextracker buys Bentek Corporation for $78 million, expands solar technology portfolio with eBOS solutions.

news.summary: Rich Okoney recently joined Cushman & Wakefield as the Global Data Center Practice Lead within the Integrated Facility Management Service Line team in Chicago. news.summary: Denver-based Spruce Power Holding Corporation, a key player in the distributed solar energy sector in the U.S., disclosed its financial performance for the first quarter ending March 31, 2025. The quarter showcased notable achievements: revenues soared to $23.8 million, marking a 30% increase from the previous year; a net loss attributable to stockholders stood at $15.3 million; and operating EBITDA climbed to $12.3 million, representing a 15% rise year-over-year. These figures reflect the company's strong performance and growth trajectory in the renewable energy market.
news.headline: Spruce Power reported Q1 2025 results: $23.8M in revenues (up 30%), net loss of $15.3M, operating EBITDA of $12.3M (up 15%).

news.summary: In Tampa, Florida, Slide Insurance has confirmed pricing for its latest catastrophe bond, Purple Re Ltd. (Series 2025-1), valued at $250 million. This marks Slide's largest cat bond offering to date, surpassing the previous record by $40 million. The Series 2025-1 bond consists of two fully collateralized classes: $125 million for Class A notes priced at 7.25% and $125 million for Class B notes priced at 7.75%. This offering demonstrates continued growth and investment in the insurance-linked securities market.
news.headline: Slide Insurance secures $250M cat bond with Purple Re Ltd. 2025-1, its largest offering to date, exceeding previous by $40M.

With a firm space background, Spire aims to leverage its capabilities for growth in these sectors.

news.summary: IREN Limited reported their financial results for the three and nine months ended March 31, 2025, with a 28% growth in profit after tax. The company is pausing mining expansion at 50 EH/s to focus on AI opportunities. In the third quarter of FY25, they had record revenues of $148.1 million, with a 24% increase in Bitcoin mining revenue to $141.2 million and a 33% increase in AI Cloud services revenue to $3.6 million. They mined 1,514 Bitcoin during this period, underpinned by growth in operating hashrate. Total net electricity costs increased to $36.5 million, while other costs remained relatively flat at $25.3 million. Cash and cash equivalents were at $184.3 million as of March 31, 2025. IREN is on track to reach 50 EH/s by June 30, 2025, and is focusing on liquid-cooled AI data centers. They are also expanding AI Cloud Services revenues and evaluating further expansion opportunities in the AI space. The company is setting the benchmark for performance in Bitcoin mining and carries out disciplined capital allocation amid macroeconomic volatility. They are transitioning to US domestic issuer status with US GAAP reporting from July 1, 2025.
news.headline: IREN Limited reported Q3 FY25 Results: $148.1M in revenues, 28% Profit After Tax growth, 50 EH/s mining expansion paused for AI focus.

news.summary: Boxlight Corporation (Nasdaq: BOXL) unveiled its financial outcomes for Q1 ended March 31, 2025, showcasing revenue of $22.4 million, marking a 39.5% drop compared to the previous year's quarter. news.headline: Boxlight Corp (Nasdaq: BOXL) reports Q1 2025 results: Revenue $22.4M (down 39.5%), Gross profit margin 35.9%, Net loss $3.2M.

news.summary: USA Rare Earth, Inc. announced its first-quarter financial results for 2025, including key highlights such as merging with Inflection Point, beginning trading on Nasdaq, opening an Innovations Lab in Stillwater, Oklahoma, hiring key employees, signing the first customer MOU for rare earth sintered magnet production in 2026, and producing dysprosium oxide from the Round Top deposit with over 99% purity. The company raised over $100 million, opened an Innovations Lab, and advanced processing technologies in Colorado. CEO Joshua Ballard emphasized the company's quick ramp-up to deliver finished magnets to customers in response to growing domestic demand. The company's growth in 2026 is expected to reflect this demand. The press release contains forward-looking statements and non-GAAP financial measures. Adjusted net loss, adjusted undistributed net loss, and adjusted undistributed net loss per common share are non-GAAP financial measures used by the company. There is information on the upcoming conference call to discuss financial results, and financial details are presented in condensed consolidated balance sheets. USA Rare Earth is developing a NdFeB magnet manufacturing plant in Stillwater, Oklahoma, aiming to establish domestic rare earth and critical minerals supply capabilities. The company's financial results showed current assets of $25.8 million and total assets of $77.1 million as of March 31, 2025. Investors can access further details on the investor relations section of the company's website.
news.headline: USA Rare Earth reported Q1 2025 financial results, including $100 million raised, 99% purity dysprosium oxide produced.

news.summary: A new partnership has been announced during the World Health Assembly, in association with the Sigmastack Climate & Health impact investing platform and Symbiotics. The collaboration between Caring Cross and the Health Innovation Exchange (HIEx) was launched in Geneva on May 14, 2025. This initiative aims to address the intersection of health and climate issues.
news.headline: Caring Cross and HIEx launch partnership to expand affordable care for cancer and sickle cell disease globally in low- and middle-income countries.
